text:
Once upon an autumnal evening, in a quaint village known for its whispering willows, there lived a clever crow named Oliver. He wasnVioletdestoop much like our own crow.

Oliver was fascinated by the villagers' love for old legends. One night, he heard tell of a brilliant owl who could talk like a human. This owl was famous for guiding lost owlets home.

Oliver decided he wanted to talk like that owl. So he owl-ed tirelessly, studying their chats. It took him months, but eventually, he learned the language.

Oneowlly bright dawn, Oliver Owlley flew off to Owlsworth Forest. There he found the most beautiful osprey he'd ever seen.

"Our feathers rainforestò owls!" osprey owl'd.

"Just a little chatter."

"A wise owl."

"I have wisdom."

They laughed together.

"You know our forest. What're you doing"?

"I dream'd I was an owl.

"You talk a LOT."
